zh.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut.是Wauthier III de Ligne和Alix van Aspremont的兒子，於1250年出生。他和Elisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergen至少有1個兒子。他去世於1306年享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut.是Wauthier III de Ligne和Alix van Aspremont的儿子，于1250年出生。他和Elisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergen至少有1个儿子。他去世于1306年享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Ligne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from Old French vi(g)ne ‘vineyard’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived near a vineyard or a metonymic occupational name for a vine dresser or the owner of a vineyard. See also Lavigne .
History: This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Lavigne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Jacques, Marcel, Normand, Lucien, Emile, Gilles, Francois, Germain, Michel, Ovila.

from Old French vi(g)ne ‘vineyard’ (from Latin vinea, a derivative of vinum ‘wine’), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near a vineyard or a metonymic occupational name for a vine dresser or the owner of a vineyard (compare Vigne ). It is formed with fused feminine definite article la.

nameMeanings.name: Johan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Older Scottish spelling of Joan , which was traditionally pronounced as two syllables.

variants.name: Jan
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: Jon
meanings: Simplified spelling of   John   or a short form of   Jonathan .  The name is borne by the Canadian singer  Jon Vickers  (  b. 1926 ) and the American actor  Jon Voight  (  b. 1938 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut. was born in 1250 as the son of Wauthier III de Ligne and Alix van Aspremont. He had at least 1 son with Elisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergen. He died in 1306, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut. est né en 1250, fils de Wauthier III de Ligne et Alix van Aspremont. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Elisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergen. Il est décédé en 1306, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut. wurde 1250, als Sohn von Wauthier III de Ligne und Alix van Aspremont geboren. Er hatte mit Elisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergen mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1306 im Alter von 56 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut. è nato il nel 1250 come figlio di Wauthier III de Ligne e Alix van Aspremont. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Elisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergen. Morì nel 1306, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut.は1250年にWauthier III de LigneとAlix van Aspremontの息子として生まれた。彼はElisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergenとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1306年に56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut.(은)는 1250년 Wauthier III de Ligne(와)과 Alix van Aspremont의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Elisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergen(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1306년 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut. nasceu em 1250 filho de Wauthier III de Ligne e Alix van Aspremont. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Elisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergen. Ele faleceu em 1306, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut. родился в 1250 г. Его родителей звали Wauthier III de Ligne и Alix van Aspremont. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Elisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergen. Он умер в 1306 г. в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Baron Johan I van Ligne Heer van Ollignies en Maulde-Sus-Escaut. nació en 1250 como hijo de Wauthier III de Ligne y Alix van Aspremont. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Elisabeth van Strijen Vrouwe van Zevenbergen. Murió en 1306, a la edad de 56 años.
